currentFocus: PatchBench online-readiness helper wave is integrated on main; the next developer slice is focused DTO-based helper-output coverage for the new PatchBench presentation helper.

publicMain.nextWriteRequirement: Before any later write, re-read live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main; do not use unmerged local campaign commits or local campaign paths as durable public state.

acceptedIntegrationEvidence.summary: Presentation-only PatchBench online-readiness helper/model extraction. BinaryPatchesPage still owns OnlineMultiplayerReadinessService calls and UI control assignment.
Tracked goal/state files no longer depend on older local-only worker/source commit hashes or campaign paths.
Roadmap index now points to the active static-to-proof rebuild/spec proof queue and non-claim boundaries.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: PatchBench helper extraction is presentation-only. AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.

pathOwnershipIncident.summary: The helper worker reported an accidental canonical static-test edit, reverted only that edit, reapplied it in the assigned worktree, and canonical main was clean before this integration merge. The final integration report records the post-integration clean status.

crossReviewNotesHandled: Path-ownership repair is preserved as a non-blocking integration note.
Future substantive PatchBench helper changes should add representative DTO-based helper-output tests that catch omitted, swapped, or boundary-losing text-state fields.
currentTruth.publicPrimaryRepo: The public repo remains the primary collaboration repo for source, docs, tools, tests, RE notes, wave notes, state batons, agent reports, readiness notes, and compact proof summaries that are non-secret and non-payload.
currentTruth.releasePublicationStatus: No release, tag, package, signing, installer, announcement, hosted workflow, or publication action is part of this integration.
currentTruth.stateBatonShape: State files should stay concise current-state handoffs. Completed campaign details belong in canonical docs/readiness notes or local reports, not live state.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs; use LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md for local-only placement.

activeSlice.name: patchbench-helper-output-dto-tests
activeSlice.goal: Add representative DTO-based tests for PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText.Build and BuildCompanionSession.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: test/helper-output coverage only; no AppCore/service behavior move, Host/Join enablement, online action enablement, release, runtime, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, patch-row, byte-patch, launch, safe-copy manifest/signature, music, gameplay, visual, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference claim changes

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration gates cover startup refs/cleanliness, git diff --check, JSON parse, stale path/hash scans, docs/repo safety, WinUI build, focused PatchBench tests, patch-engine safety, hard-payload safety, and primary-lane validation.
validation.nextSlice: Use focused helper-output tests plus relevant PatchBench/WinUI and repo safety gates; keep all online non-claims intact.

nextSteps: Before the next write, verify live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main.
Add representative DTO-based tests for the PatchBench online-readiness helper before any substantive helper-output change.

Keep package scripts, release publication, Host/Join, online readiness, runtime audio, gameplay, visual, rebuild, no-noticeable-difference, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, and original-BEA mutation claims bounded to accepted evidence.
